compute.cost_note: embeddings; CPU-feasible but slow, GPU optional.

do_not_use_when: You want an AI/human verdict (this surface ships uncalibrated and refuses one)
You need values comparable across embedding models (PHD is embedding-model dependent)
Target is short (too few embedding units destabilizes the scaling fit)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

purpose: Clean-room persistent-homology dimension (PHD) of a text's contextual-embedding point cloud — a topological discrimination signal reported in the literature as the single most orthogonal axis to perplexity / lexical signals (Tulchinskii et al. 2023). Human prose tends to a higher intrinsic dimension than AI prose. Emits the scalar PHD plus sample size and the embedding-model id; uncalibrated (no band, no verdict) and the claim-license refuses an AI/human verdict absent operator-supplied thresholds. On short text (n_points < 200, short_text_mode="auto") it routes to the Short-PHD bootstrap-aggregation path (arXiv:2504.02873): the PHD is the median over seeded sub-cloud fits with an explicit stability spread (fit.phd_stability), an honest interval instead of one fragile point — long-text output is unchanged.
use_when: You want a geometry-based discrimination axis orthogonal to perplexity / lexical signals
You're assembling a multi-signal evidence pack and want a topological reading
Target is >= 500 words (enough embedding units to fit the PHD scaling law)
